title: The Understudy WIP
description: Work-in-progress build of The Understudy, a new playable character for Slay the Spire 2. Shared for friend testing. Requires BaseLib.
changeNote: v1.11.35 - Composure and Comeback no longer glow gold when the only Unplayable cards in hand are statuses/curses (e.g. Infection). They only free Unplayable attacks and skills, so they now glow only when there is actually one to free. --- v1.11.34 - Internal cleanup: renamed the code classes to match their display names (Best of Both, Shameful Gift, Hard Earned Feat) that had been left with their old names. Their internal ids changed to match, so a card/relic in an in-progress run may not carry over — start a fresh run if you had them. --- v1.11.33 - Duet now resolves exactly like Best of Both for the targeted teammate (simultaneous Swap + Invert on each of their debuffs, plus stealing each enemy's buff), via shared code so the two can't drift. --- v1.11.32 - 'Give & Take' renamed to 'Best of Both' and now also steals each enemy's buff again (the Swap 'take' half was mistakenly dropped last patch, so you weren't getting the enemy's Vigor). Invert/Swap keyword tooltips simplified. Relics renamed: 'Constant Struggle' -> 'Shameful Gift', 'Constant Growth' -> 'Hard Earned Feat'. --- v1.11.31 - Simplified the Swap keyword tooltip to 'Trade a debuff of yours with a buff from each enemy. Up to 10 of each.' --- v1.11.30 - 'Give and Take' renamed to 'Give & Take' and reworked so Swap and Invert resolve simultaneously: for each of your debuffs, both effects read the same starting amount — Invert flips up to {Invert} of it into a buff on you AND Swap pushes it onto every enemy, then the debuff is stripped once. So from 1 Weak you now become Unweak AND apply Weak to all enemies (previously Swap consumed your Weak before Invert could flip it). Sign-flip debuffs (negative Vigor/Strength/Dexterity) are inverted on you only; the card no longer steals enemy buffs. --- v1.11.29 - One-up base damage lowered from 2 to 1 per hit (it hits 4 times, 5 upgraded). --- v1.11.28 - One-up now hits 4 times by default and 5 when upgraded (up from 3 / 4). --- v1.11.27 - Calculated previews (Fate Knocking's finisher damage, Clean Slate & Loosen Up's hit count) now show only during combat, matching the base game — no longer in the Compendium. Fate Knocking: no longer Stable, costs 2, and upgrade now raises its base damage from 1 to 2. Loosen Up: hits a single chosen enemy (no longer random), once per Unplayable in hand. Strike a Pose: now hits a random enemy 3 times for 4 (was 2 hits of 3 on a single target), still Inverts — differentiating it from Comeback. --- v1.11.26 - Pre-Planned cards now take unique, deck-ordered Planned indexes at combat start (no more shared placeholder slot), so they play in a fixed order = their position in the deck. New Uncommon relics: Score (you may Enchant a card with Planned at Rest Sites) and Foldable Stage (you may Enchant a card with Tuned at Rest Sites); the enchantment rides the card across all combats, so it starts each combat pre-Planned / pre-Tuned. New relics, enchantments, and rest-site options currently use placeholder art. --- v1.11.25 - Adds the character's first 8 Potions (Invert, Swap, Planned, Tuned, Milkshake, Love, Protein, Vigor) and 5 Relics: Rosin (apply an extra Tuned the first time a card is Tuned), Lozenge (gain 2 Vigor at the start of each turn), Safety Net (start each combat with 1 Unvulnerable and 1 Unfrail), Cue Light (every 5 cards that become Unplayable, apply 2 Weak to a random enemy), and Greasepaint (every 5 debuffs applied to you, apply 2 Vulnerable to a random enemy). Potions/relics currently use placeholder art.
